nodes.label: P3.1 — cross-layer coordination (Direction 1 / OLU)
nodes.description: Coordination window w in {1,2,4} on the contrastive cell (SCFFContrastOLU): layers trained in joint groups, gradient shared then detached. w=1 reproduces P3.0 contrast exactly (regression guard). CIFAR-flat headline + synth sanity.

nodes.label: coordination window w (Direction 1 / OLU)
nodes.description: A layer is trained to help the next layer's discrimination (the user's Direction 1), supplied forward-only via a joint-group InfoNCE with gradient shared within the window then detached at window boundaries. w=1=no coordination (myopic); w=2 the cheapest sufficient; larger w helps more where task headroom is large.
nodes.rationale: Converts 'preserved' class info into 'composed' (rising) class info; the decisive lever in P3.2's headroom confirmation.

nodes.label: P3.1 verdict: PARTIAL — coordination is real+correct but flat-CIFAR has no headroom for anyone
nodes.description: CIFAR: w1=-0.0158 -> w2=-0.0105 (~33% flatter, deep endpoint 0.230->0.254, selectivity +0.066) -> w4 saturates (-0.0113). Synth (headroom exists): w1=-0.005 -> w2=+0.002 -> w4=+0.008 (RISES). Diagnostic unlock: GD-hidden is itself flat (~0.36) on CIFAR-flat, so slope>=0 was the wrong bar there -- a task-ceiling artifact, not a method failure.

nodes.label: Decision: lock contrast+w2 -> build a headroom task -> route P3.2
nodes.description: Since flat-CIFAR can't show 'rising' for any method, the achievable static win (stop destruction, preserve class, degrade gently) is met. Route to building a depth-headroom synthetic task so the composing claim is actually testable, before the continual veto.
